title: Underpowered but direction-matched: HLA-A pos-224 (5'UTR -155 from ATG) CG vs TG partition shows lower TPM at CG (Cohen's d ~ -0.93)

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Underpowered but direction-matched: HLA-A pos-224 (5'UTR -155 from ATG) CG vs TG'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Bettens 2022 TPM, HLA-A subset. n=19 protein-distinct alleles after dropping alleles with missing pos-224 dinucleotide call.
Dinucleotide identity at HLA-A 5'UTR position 145 (= -155 from ATG = canonical 'pos-224' in the Ramsuran 2015 numbering).

operations.note: Pre-specified directional test: CG TPM less than TG TPM (Ramsuran 2015 prediction: CG at pos-224 is methylation target, lowers expression).
Phase 6B robustness: trim top and bottom 5% of TPM values per group, recompute d. Ensures the effect is not driven by single tail alleles.

statistics.name: cohens_d_cg_minus_tg_tail_excluded
statistics.note: Phase 6B: similar magnitude after tail exclusion -> effect is not single-allele-driven.

statistics.name: mwu_p_one_sided_cg_less_tg
statistics.note: p = 0.13 — does not pass conventional alpha=0.05 even one-sided. n=19 with 5 vs 14 imbalance limits power.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts encode a *qualified* support: (1) directional sign matches Ramsuran 2015 prediction (CG -> lower TPM); (2) effect magnitude exceeds Cohen's 'medium' threshold (|d| > 0.5) so the signal is not negligible; (3) tail-excluded magnitude also exceeds 0.5 so the result is not single-allele-driven. The Mann-Whitney U p = 0.13 is NOT in the conjunction — including it would falsify a SUPPORTED claim that the analysis is meant to register as 'direction-matched but underpowered,' which is the qualified-positive register.
inference.failure_mode: If s_cohens_d_full flipped sign or fell below |0.5|, the Ramsuran prediction would not be direction-matched in this sample. If s_cohens_d_tail_excluded fell below |0.5|, the effect would be tail-driven. Either failure would dissolve the qualified claim.

conclusion.assertion: Among 19 HLA-A protein-distinct alleles in the Bettens 2022 cohort, partitioning by dinucleotide identity at 5'UTR position 145 (Ramsuran 2015 'pos-224') yields direction-matched lower expression at the CG dinucleotide vs TG (Cohen's d = -0.93, robust to tail exclusion at d = -0.98). The Mann-Whitney U p = 0.13 is consistent with the n=19 / 5-vs-14 imbalance not affording conventional significance; the claim is registered as direction-matched but underpowered.

scope.scale_note: Single-position test at pos 145 in HLA-A. Generalization to other 5'UTR positions and to other loci is encoded as separate claims (see HLA-B claim sibling).

confidence.note: Underpowered: n=19 with 5/14 split. Outcome registered as qualified_positive to surface this in the latent space.

external_assumptions.statement: Ramsuran et al. 2015 mechanistic model — CG at 5'UTR pos-224 is a CpG methylation target that downregulates HLA-A — is a defensible prior for this directional test.

5'UTR position numbering: pos 145 in our IPD-IMGT 5'UTR coordinate system corresponds to -155 bp from the ATG, which corresponds to Ramsuran 'pos-224' in the alternative coordinate system of that paper.
Underpowered direction-matched effects are scientifically informative when the prior is strong (Ramsuran). The qualified_positive outcome registers this without overclaiming.
